product_brief: Open QKD platform designed as a versatile research tool for academic and technology evaluation labs.
pqc_support: No (Document describes Quantum Key Distribution, not Post-Quantum Cryptography algorithms)
pqc_capability_description: The document states the product is a Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) system providing "proven secrecy of encryption keys" via quantum physics principles. It mentions protecting against future quantum computers by securing key exchange, but does not state support for specific PQC algorithms (e.g., lattice-based, hash-based).
crypto_primitives: AES-256 (mentioned in context of key rate: "14'000 AES-256 Keys per hour"); QKD (Quantum Key Distribution)
key_management_model: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) for secure key generation and distribution; integrated with Clarion KX software suite for network and key management.
architecture_type: Open QKD platform; 19" rackmount 1U size appliance (inferred from XG Series description which shares architecture); designed for complex network topologies (ring, hub and spoke, meshed, star).
latest_version: 4th generation (Cerberis XGR is the second model in the XG series)
release_date: 2021-10-18 (Press release date: "18th October 2021 IDQ unveils Cerberis XGR QKD Platform")
primary_platforms: Academia, Research institutes, Innovation labs; compatible with major Ethernet and OTN encryptors.
target_industries: Academia, Research institutes, Innovation labs, Government & Defense, Telecommunications, Banking & Finance, Critical Infrastructure, Healthcare, IP Protection.
regulatory_status: Not stated (Company has ISO 14001 certification, but no specific product regulatory licenses mentioned)
pqc_roadmap_details: Not stated (Product is QKD, not PQC software migration)
